categories.title: Control of Vehicle
categories.description: Driving technique, braking, steering and moving off safely.

topics.title: Steering and Control
topics.description: Maintaining smooth control of the car.

categories.title: Rules of the Road
categories.description: Traffic signs, signals and road markings.

categories.title: Managing Risk
categories.description: Hazard awareness, spacing and overtaking judgement.

categories.title: Safe and Responsible Driving
categories.description: Sharing the road and driving with care.

categories.title: Technical Matters
categories.description: Vehicle warning lights, tyres and core systems.

questions.prompt: What steering method gives the best control in normal driving?
questions.explanation: Push-pull steering keeps both hands in control and avoids crossing arms over the wheel.

options.text: Turn quickly with one hand whenever possible
Use push-pull steering with both hands
Hold the wheel from inside the rim
Let the wheel slide freely through your hands

questions.prompt: How should you brake for a smooth, controlled stop?
questions.explanation: Progressive braking gives following traffic more warning and keeps the car balanced.

options.text: Brake progressively and ease off near the stop
Brake late and firmly at the last moment
Apply the handbrake before using the foot brake
Pump the brake pedal repeatedly

questions.prompt: Before moving off from the roadside, what matters most?
questions.explanation: You should check mirrors and blind spots so you only move when it is safe to join traffic.

options.text: Move quickly while signalling
Sound the horn and edge out
Check mirrors and blind spot before moving
Rev the engine so others hear you

questions.prompt: What should you do at an amber traffic light if it is safe to stop?
questions.explanation: Amber means stop if you can do so safely before the stop line.

options.text: Accelerate through before it turns red
Continue unless a pedestrian is crossing
Stop only if you see a camera
Stop before the line

questions.prompt: What does a continuous white centre line generally mean?
questions.explanation: A continuous centre line normally means you must not cross or straddle it except where specifically allowed.

options.text: Overtaking is encouraged
Do not cross or straddle it unless permitted
Parking is allowed at all times
Traffic from the left has priority

questions.prompt: What should you do when a yellow box junction is occupied?
questions.explanation: You should only enter when your exit is clear so you do not block the junction.

options.text: Enter if your light is green
Enter and wait in the box
Wait until your exit is clear
Sound the horn and proceed slowly

questions.prompt: How should you adjust following distance in heavy rain?
questions.explanation: Wet roads increase stopping distance, so you need a larger safety gap.

options.text: Increase the gap to allow longer stopping distance
Keep the same gap as dry weather
Reduce the gap so others cannot cut in
Drive close and brake early

questions.prompt: If you begin overtaking and then see oncoming traffic closing quickly, what should you do?
questions.explanation: The safest option is to abandon the overtake and return to your lane as soon as it is safe.

options.text: Accelerate hard and force completion
Brake sharply while staying in the oncoming lane
Return safely to your lane as soon as possible
Flash your headlights and continue

questions.prompt: Approaching a blind bend, what is the safest action?
questions.explanation: Reducing speed and holding a safe lane position gives you time to respond to hazards you cannot yet see.

options.text: Move toward the centre for a better view
Reduce speed and keep proper lane position
Overtake before entering the bend
Use full beam to warn others

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, what should you do?
questions.explanation: You should move safely aside and allow a clear passage without creating another hazard.

options.text: Brake suddenly where you are
Move safely aside and let it pass
Speed up to stay ahead of it
Block the junction so others stop too

questions.prompt: If you feel very tired while driving, what should you do?
questions.explanation: Fatigue reduces awareness and reaction time. Stop in a safe place and rest rather than pushing on.

options.text: Open the window and keep going
Drink coffee and continue without stopping
Drive faster so the trip ends sooner
Stop in a safe place and rest

questions.prompt: Approaching a pedestrian crossing with people waiting, how should you respond?
questions.explanation: You should slow down and be ready to stop for pedestrians who may begin crossing.

options.text: Slow down and be prepared to stop
Continue if they have not stepped out yet
Sound the horn so they wait
Pass quickly before they cross

questions.prompt: A red brake warning light stays on while driving. What should you do?
questions.explanation: A red brake warning may indicate a serious fault. Stop safely and investigate immediately.

options.text: Ignore it until the next service
Drive faster to clear the warning
Stop safely and investigate immediately
Switch the lights off and on again

questions.prompt: What can under-inflated tyres cause?
questions.explanation: Under-inflated tyres reduce stability and can increase braking distance and wear.

options.text: Better fuel economy
Poor handling and longer braking distance
Improved cornering grip
No change to vehicle behaviour

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of ABS brakes?
questions.explanation: ABS helps prevent wheel lock so the driver can maintain steering control under heavy braking.

options.text: Prevent wheel lock during hard braking
Increase top speed
Reduce fuel consumption
Shorten stopping distance on every surface

questions.prompt: When driving downhill for a long distance, what helps you keep better control?
questions.explanation: Selecting a lower gear uses engine braking and reduces the need to overuse the foot brake.

options.text: Stay in a high gear and brake hard
Select a lower gear to use engine braking
Shift to neutral to roll freely
Use the handbrake lightly while moving

questions.prompt: If your car begins to skid, what should you do first?
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering gently into the skid helps the tyres regain grip.

options.text: Brake as hard as possible
Accelerate sharply
Ease off the accelerator and steer gently into the skid
Pull the handbrake immediately

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to bring your car to a stop on a wet road?
questions.explanation: Wet roads reduce grip, so smooth progressive braking gives the tyres more chance to hold the road.

options.text: Brake late to keep traffic flowing
Pump the pedal quickly
Use the handbrake before the foot brake
Brake early and progressively

questions.prompt: A circular sign with a red border and the number 50 usually means:
questions.explanation: A circular sign with a red border shows a mandatory limit or prohibition. With a number, it is a speed limit.

options.text: A recommended speed of 50 km/h
A minimum speed of 50 km/h
A speed limit of 50 km/h
Road number 50

questions.prompt: When is overtaking on the left allowed in slow-moving traffic?
questions.explanation: In slow-moving traffic, vehicles in a lane may pass those in another lane on the left if traffic in that lane is moving more quickly.

options.text: Whenever you are in a hurry
When traffic in your lane is moving faster
Only on the hard shoulder
Never under any circumstances

questions.prompt: What should you do if a lane arrow shows that your lane must turn left?
questions.explanation: Lane arrows give advance instruction. You should follow the lane direction shown unless road signs or markings indicate otherwise.

options.text: Follow the lane and turn left
Continue straight if traffic is light
Stop in the lane until it clears
Ignore it if your indicator is on

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what is the safest sequence?
questions.explanation: Use mirrors and a blind-spot check before moving, and signal in time so other road users understand your intention.

options.text: Signal and move immediately
Check the centre mirror only
Increase speed and then look
Mirror, signal, blind-spot check, then move if safe

questions.prompt: Why should you leave extra space when following a motorcycle?
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can be affected more by surface hazards, wind, and sudden changes in balance, so extra space gives you more time to react.

options.text: They always stop more slowly than cars
They may need to react suddenly to road hazards
They are required to travel more slowly
They cannot use mirrors properly

questions.prompt: At night, when should you dip your headlights?
questions.explanation: Dip your headlights when meeting or following other road users so you do not dazzle them.

options.text: Only in towns
Only when it is raining
When meeting or following other road users
Never on national roads

questions.prompt: When passing a cyclist, what is best practice?
questions.explanation: Leave as much safe space as possible and only overtake when you can do so without squeezing the cyclist.

options.text: Pass close to stop them wobbling
Leave as much safe space as possible
Use the horn as you pass
Only pass if the cyclist waves you through

questions.prompt: What is the safest mobile phone policy while driving?
questions.explanation: Even hands-free use can distract you. The safest option is to use the phone only when fully stopped in a safe place.

options.text: Use it briefly at red lights
Use speaker mode in light traffic
Text only on straight roads
Use it only when fully stopped in a safe place

questions.prompt: Approaching a school crossing, what should you expect?
questions.explanation: Children may act unpredictably near schools, so you should reduce speed and be prepared to stop quickly and safely.

options.text: Children may step out unexpectedly
The crossing will only be used at lunch time
You can maintain speed if no one is on the road
Only cyclists have priority there

questions.prompt: If the engine temperature warning indicates overheating, what should you do?
questions.explanation: Overheating can seriously damage the engine. Stop safely and switch off when appropriate before investigating.

options.text: Keep driving so airflow cools the engine
Stop safely and switch off the engine when appropriate
Pour cold water into the hot engine immediately
Ignore it if the car is still moving

questions.prompt: Why should tyre tread depth be checked regularly?
questions.explanation: Good tread depth helps the tyre maintain grip, especially in wet conditions, and reduces the risk of skidding.

options.text: To improve stereo performance
To reduce steering wheel size
To maintain grip, especially in wet conditions
To increase engine power

questions.prompt: What should you do if a warning light appears and you are unsure what it means?
questions.explanation: Unknown warning lights should never be ignored. Check the vehicle handbook and stop safely if the warning suggests a serious fault.

options.text: Ignore it until the next service
Drive faster so the engine clears it
Turn the radio off and on
Check the handbook and stop safely if needed
